rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical intramolecular oxy-chalcogenation of N-allylbenzamides and allyl oximes with diaryl dichalcogenides to form selenylated/thiolated oxazolines and isoxazolines via tandem C-O and C-chalcogen bond formation
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of PhSeSePh (peak 1.66 V vs Ag/AgCl) to PhSe+ cation and PhSe radical; also direct anodic oxidation of the amide substrate (1.32 V) to a delocalized N/O-centered radical; cathodic reduction generates H2 and reduces PhSeSePh (-1.44 V) to PhSe radical + PhSe anion (oxidized back to PhSe radical)

key_intermediates.name: seleniranium/β-selenyl carbocation C from PhSe+ addition to allyl alkene
delocalized amidyl/oxy radical E from oxidation of N-allylbenzamide 1a
TEMPO adduct 13 from trapping of allyl/carbon radical of oxime 6a

electron_flow: At the anode, PhSeSePh is oxidized to PhSe+ (cation) and PhSe• (radical), while the amide/oxime substrate can also be directly oxidized to a heteroatom-centered radical; PhSe+ adds to the allyl alkene to give a cation cyclized by the amide/oxime oxygen (ionic pathway I), or the substrate-derived radical cyclizes and couples with PhSe• (radical pathway II); the cathode reduces protons to H2 and PhSeSePh to PhSe•/PhSe-.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO (with amide 1a)
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3aa formation diminished to 21% yield, indicating partial radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO (with oxime 6a)
probe_reagents.observation: Selenylation shut down (only trace of 7aa); TEMPO-carbon adduct 13 isolated in 78% yield, supporting radical intermediate for oxime

probe_reagents.name: Controlled potential electrolysis at 1.00, 1.55, 1.77 V (0.5 F/mol)
probe_reagents.observation: No product at 1.00 V; 27% 3aa at 1.55 V (only reduction of 2a + oxidation of 1a feasible → radical pathway active); 39% at 1.77 V exceeds the 33% maximum for pathway II alone, indicating ionic pathway I also operates

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: PhSeSePh oxidation peak at 1.66 V and reduction peak at -1.44 V vs Ag/AgCl; amide 1a oxidation at 1.32 V; oxime 6a oxidation at 1.13 V (lower, consistent with dominant radical pathway for isoxazolines)

notes: Two parallel mechanisms proposed: Pathway I (ionic, PhSe+ addition then O-cyclization, 1.0 F/mol) and Pathway II (radical, substrate oxidation/cyclization then PhSe• coupling, 1.5 F/mol). For oxazolines from amides both pathways operate; for isoxazolines from oximes the radical pathway dominates as evidenced by the high yield of TEMPO adduct 13 and the lower oxidation potential of the oxime. Only sub-stoichiometric diselenide is needed because PhSe is recycled between anode and cathode.
